meta.question: Compose a meta-skill for an analyst workflow combining web research, PDF intelligence, and final docx export into a reusable due-diligence brief workflow

subquestions.question: What is the canonical SKILL.md structure and frontmatter contract (name/description rules, directory layout, three-level loading)?
What tools are available for web research automation (web_search, web_fetch, subagent patterns in AgentOS)?
subquestions.notes: OpenClaw docs for web tools and sub-agents

subquestions.question: What Python libraries are available for PDF intelligence (extraction, table parsing, markdown conversion)?
How can python-docx / docxtpl be used to generate formatted Word documents programmatically?
What due diligence report structure is standard (sections, executive summary, financial/legal/operational analysis)?
What are collision risks and gate decisions when composing multiple capabilities (fetch races, partial failures, state corruption)?
subquestions.notes: Best practices from automation orchestration docs

subquestions.question: What triggers and inputs should the meta-skill expose (user phrases, file references, parameters)?
subquestions.notes: Skill authoring best practices for description design

subquestions.question: What step-graph should the meta-skill define (parallel branches, sequential gates, feedback loops)?
subquestions.notes: Subagent orchestration patterns from OpenClaw docs
